config_files.notes: Single flat INI file covering all graphics, display, input, and audio options. Note the space in the folder name ('Skull And Bones'). Contains a [HARDWARE_INFO] section written by the in-game benchmark — do not edit those values. The [LightingDevices] section controls RGB peripheral integration; do not edit unless disabling RGB.
Also accessible via Xbox Game Pass for PC. Path is the same as other PC versions.

keys.effect: Frame rate ceiling in FPS. The in-game slider only allows values up to 90, but editing this key directly in the config file accepts higher values (e.g. 120, 144).
keys.notes: Must be set alongside IsFpsLimitEnabled=1 to take effect. Values above 90 cannot be set via the in-game UI — config file editing is required. Setting above the display's native refresh rate provides no benefit and wastes GPU power. DLSS 3 Frame Generation (DynamicResolutionFrameGeneration=1) is recommended alongside this cap to help reach and sustain 120+ FPS at high quality settings.

keys.effect: Enables (1) or disables (0) the MaxFPS frame rate limiter.
keys.notes: Default is 0 (disabled), leaving the game uncapped. Always enable for consistent frame pacing — especially important in a nautical game where variable frame times amplify perceived motion from waves and ship pitch.

keys.effect: In-game VSync. 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Disable in-game VSync and use G-Sync or FreeSync instead to avoid input latency penalty.

keys.effect: Texture quality. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Very High/Ultra.
keys.notes: In-game auto-detect may set this to 1 (Medium) even on high-VRAM GPUs. On GPUs with 8 GB+ VRAM at 1080p–1440p, and 12 GB+ at ultrawide resolutions, value 3 is appropriate for a quality goal.

keys.effect: Lighting quality, affecting dynamic sky, ocean atmosphere, and ship illumination. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High.
keys.notes: In-game auto-detect may erroneously set this to 0 (Low) on capable hardware. Low lighting substantially degrades the game's signature ocean and sky atmosphere. High (2) is the recommended quality target.

keys.effect: Water simulation and rendering quality including wave geometry, foam, and surface detail. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Ultra.
keys.notes: Water is the primary visual feature of Skull and Bones. Highest priority quality setting for this game. Ultra (3) recommended on all mid-range and above GPUs with 8 GB+ VRAM.

keys.effect: Screen-space reflection quality on water and ship surfaces. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High.
keys.notes: High-impact setting for ocean visual quality. Consider pairing with RayTraceGI=1 for best combined result on RTX GPUs.

keys.effect: Shadow rendering quality. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Ultra.
Screen-space ambient occlusion quality. Adds contact shadows and depth to scene geometry. Known values: 0=Off, 1=Low/Medium, 2=High.
Island and port terrain rendering quality. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High.
Geometry tessellation quality for terrain and water surfaces. Known values: 0=Off, 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High/Ultra.
General environment quality including foliage, distant objects, and environmental detail. Known values: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Very High, 4=Ultra.
Anisotropic texture filtering quality. Known values: 0=Low (bilinear), 1=Medium (4x AF), 2=High (8x–16x AF).
Ray traced global illumination. 0=Off, 1=On. Requires DirectX 12 and an RTX or RDNA2+ GPU.
keys.notes: Highest performance cost of any single setting. For quality goal: enable on RTX 30/40-series or RDNA3+. If FPS drops below target with all other quality settings applied, this is the first setting to disable. Pairs well with DLSS 3 Frame Generation on RTX 40-series.

keys.effect: DLSS 3 Frame Generation (NVIDIA RTX 40-series only). 0=Off, 1=On. Inserts AI-generated frames to double effective output framerate.
keys.notes: Enable on RTX 40-series for all goals — it significantly increases output FPS with minimal visual overhead, enabling higher quality settings elsewhere. Has no effect on non-RTX 40-series GPUs.

keys.effect: Chromatic aberration (peripheral colour fringing effect). 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Recommended Off for all goals. Particularly pronounced during ship turning and broadside impacts. Primary motion sickness trigger with no gameplay value.

keys.effect: Motion blur. 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Recommended Off for all goals. Amplifies perceived motion sickness in a nautical game with constant ship movement.

keys.effect: Film grain overlay. 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Recommended Off for all goals. Persistent noise reduces perceived image quality at high resolutions.

keys.effect: Depth of field effect. 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Recommended Off for all goals. Inconsistent focal plane during constant wave motion causes visual fatigue and eye strain. No meaningful visual benefit in open-world gameplay.

keys.effect: High dynamic range output. 0=Off, 1=On.
keys.notes: Enable if display supports HDR. The game's ocean and sky environments benefit substantially from HDR luminance range.

keys.effect: Field of view multiplier relative to the game's default FOV. Range: 0.85 (85%) to 1.15 (115%). Corresponds to the in-game FOV slider.
keys.notes: For ultrawide (21:9 or 32:9) displays, always pair with AutoFOV=1. At 1.15 (maximum) with AutoFOV=1, the game correctly scales FOV for the aspect ratio. Without AutoFOV=1, this value is applied relative to a 16:9 baseline regardless of actual aspect ratio.

keys.effect: Automatic FOV scaling based on display aspect ratio. 0=Off (manual FOVScale applied against 16:9 baseline), 1=On (FOVScale applied against the actual aspect ratio of the display).
keys.notes: Recommended On (1) for all goals, especially critical on ultrawide (21:9/32:9) displays. With AutoFOV=0 on a 32:9 display, the effective horizontal FOV is narrower than the FOVScale value implies, amplifying perceived motion and motion sickness. Set to 1 to allow the engine to compute correct FOV for the actual screen geometry.

keys.effect: Controls HUD layout on ultrawide displays. 0=HUD locked to 16:9 centre, 1=HUD fills the full ultrawide screen width.
keys.notes: Personal preference. Value 1 (fill screen) is recommended for ultrawide users to use the full display. Value 0 keeps HUD elements in the 16:9 safe area, which some users prefer to reduce eye travel. Note: menus and cutscenes are pillarboxed regardless of this setting (PCGamingWiki confirmed).

keys.effect: Display window mode. 2=Borderless Fullscreen.
keys.notes: Borderless fullscreen (2) is the only fullscreen option available in Skull and Bones per PCGamingWiki. Exclusive fullscreen is not supported.

engine_overrides.behaviour: The in-game FPS limiter slider caps at 90 FPS maximum. However, editing MaxFPS directly in the config file bypasses this ceiling and accepts any positive integer value.
engine_overrides.action: Set MaxFPS to the desired value (e.g. 120, 144) directly in the config file. The in-game slider will show 90 as maximum but the config value takes precedence. Always set IsFpsLimitEnabled=1 alongside.

manual_only_settings.reason: Ship impacts, broadside cannon fire, and storm conditions trigger camera shake. Not exposed as a config key. Particularly significant motion sickness trigger in a naval combat game.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: DLSS Quality on NVIDIA RTX; FSR Quality on AMD; TAA on other GPUs
manual_only_settings.reason: The interaction between the config keys Antialiasing and SuperResolution is not fully documented. Changing AA mode via the in-game menu is safer than direct config editing. DLSS/FSR can only be used with upscaling enabled — there is no native-resolution DLSS AA option per PCGamingWiki.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Select appropriate mode: Deuteranopia/Protanopia or Tritanopia
manual_only_settings.reason: Colour blind mode is available in-game but not exposed as a config key.

notes: Always-online DRM required for all game modes (Ubisoft Connect + Denuvo + VMProtect).
Borderless fullscreen is the only fullscreen mode — exclusive fullscreen is not supported.
Menus and cutscenes are pillarboxed on ultrawide displays regardless of WideAspectStretchedHUDMode.
DLSS 3 Frame Generation (DynamicResolutionFrameGeneration=1) is available on RTX 40-series and is highly recommended alongside RayTraceGI=1 to offset the RT performance cost.
The in-game FPS limiter slider caps at 90 FPS. Values above 90 can only be set by editing MaxFPS directly in the config file.
Quality value scale for most settings: 0=Low, 1=Medium, 2=High, 3=Very High/Ultra, 4=Ultra (for Environment). Confirmed by observed config values from auto-detect benchmark.
The [HARDWARE_INFO] section (CPUScore, GPUScore, ConfidenceLevelCPU, ConfidenceLevelGPU) is written by the in-game auto-detect benchmark. Do not edit these values manually.
In-game auto-detect may set Lighting=0 and Texture=1 (both at minimum) even on high-end hardware. Always verify these after running auto-detect.
